contract.name: v2/agency/toptier_code/obligations_by_award_category.md
contract.description: Breaks down an agency's award obligations by category for a selected fiscal year, powering USAspending agency profile visualizations.

toptier_code.description: Numeric identifier for the top-tier agency whose obligations are requested.
toptier_code.constraints: Required. Observed to accept only digit strings of length 3 or 4. Known codes return data; unknown digit strings return a JSON 404; non-numeric strings miss the route and return an HTML 404 from the web server.

fiscal_year.description: Fiscal year to aggregate obligations for the selected agency.
fiscal_year.constraints: Optional. Accepts integers from 2008 through 2026 inclusive. Omitting the parameter or sending an empty string defaults to the latest dataset (FY 2026 as of 2026-02-13). Non-integer inputs return 400 validation errors; integers outside the range return 422 with explicit bound messaging.

total_aggregated_amount.description: Total obligations summed across all categories; observed to equal the sum of the results entries and to be zero or positive depending on agency data.

results.description: Always returns six category subtotals ordered as [contracts, direct_payments, grants, idvs, loans, other], even when values are zero or negative.

category.description: Award category label; response order is fixed as listed.

aggregated_amount.description: Total obligations for the category; values may be positive, zero, or negative (e.g., loan adjustments).

body.detail: Agency with a toptier code of '999' does not exist

quirks: Results array order is fixed by category label rather than aggregated_amount despite documentation claiming descending sort.
Every award category is returned even when the aggregated amount is zero or negative, contradicting the documentation's omission rule.
Loans subtotals can be negative, so totals may rely on offsets rather than strictly positive obligations.
Non-numeric toptier codes route to a generic HTML 404 page instead of the API's JSON error envelope.
risks: Loans aggregated_amount values can be negative, which may surprise clients expecting strictly positive totals.
Non-numeric toptier codes trigger an HTML 404 instead of JSON, so clients should not assume uniform error bodies.
Fiscal year bounds are enforced (2008-2026) and likely to change as new data releases; integrations should handle future expansions.

probes.notes: fail: Default request returns FY 2026 data but results are not sorted descending and include zero/negative categories.
pass: Setting fiscal_year=2023 returns FY 2023 totals with all categories present.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"detail":"Invalid value in 'fiscal_year'. 'abcd' is not a valid type (integer)"}

probes.notes: pass: Non-integer fiscal_year string is rejected with 400 validation detail.
pass: Upper fiscal_year bound is currently 2026; requesting 2027 yields 422 with explicit max messaging.

probes.notes: fail: Non-numeric toptier code hits a generic HTML 404 instead of JSON error payload.
pass: Zero-obligation agency still returns all six categories with zero totals, contradicting doc guidance about omission.
pass: 4-digit numeric toptier_code returns data with the documented schema, confirming accepted length.

mismatches: Documentation states results are sorted by aggregated_amount descending, but live responses preserve a fixed category order.
Documentation states categories with $0 obligations are omitted, but zero and negative categories are still present in every response.
risks: Loans aggregated_amount values can be negative, which may surprise clients expecting strictly positive totals.
Non-numeric toptier codes trigger an HTML 404 instead of JSON, so clients should not assume uniform error bodies.
Fiscal year bounds are enforced (2008-2026) and likely to change as new data releases; integrations should handle future expansions.
